commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Maintainers should confirm the intended legacy-profile compatibility break and wait for the supported-surface gates; this review did not find a narrow automated repair target.; Cleared: The diff changes local Nix module wiring, wrapper environment, and checks without adding new dependency sources, secret exposure, or broader runtime permissions beyond declared PATH injection. (sha=dad7cfa925588b79e9fd9e0a5cb0e581cb5ce0f1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR routes Nix-managed runtime package bin paths into generated OpenClaw exec and Codex app-server PATH configuration, removes the legacy Codex-home profile linker, and adds runtime-path smoke coverage.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence runtime reproduction was run in this read-only review. Source inspection shows current main still wires runtimePackages through the legacy Codex-home profile activation and does not render them into tools.exec.pathPrepend.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers should confirm the intended legacy-profile compatibility break and wait for the supported-surface gates; this review did not find a narrow automated repair target.

target.title: Fix OpenClaw command tool runtime path
target.body:
for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

## User Problem

Nix users declare OpenClaw command-line tools with `programs.openclaw.runtimePackages`, but OpenClaw/Codex command execution could still run without those tools on `PATH` and fail with `command not found`.

The old Nix-side workaround linked packages into each agent's isolated Codex home at `agents/<id>/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin`. That was an internal filesystem layout, not the current OpenClaw command-execution contract. OpenClaw already exposes `tools.exec.pathPrepend` for command tools, and Codex app-server command execution can receive the same path through its existing `shell_environment_policy.set.PATH` config.

## What This Changes

This PR makes `runtimePackages` mean one thing: Nix-managed command tools for OpenClaw command execution.

It does that by:

- adding runtime package bin paths to the generated gateway wrapper `PATH`;
- rendering the same bin paths into global and agent-level `tools.exec.pathPrepend`;
- passing the same bin path to the Codex app-server through existing `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS` / `shell_environment_policy.set.PATH`;
- using `lib.getBin` so split-output packages point at their `bin` output;
- removing the legacy Codex-home profile linker.

The PR intentionally does **not** export `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN`. OpenClaw still owns the managed Codex app-server binary lifecycle; nix-openclaw only supplies the command-tool PATH.

## Compatibility

User config stays the same:

```nix
programs.openclaw.runtimePackages = [ pkgs.jq ];
```

The compatibility break is limited to the old internal Codex-home profile path. Pre-1.0 deployments that depended on `agents/<id>/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin` should depend on `runtimePackages` / `tools.exec.pathPrepend` instead. There is no cleanup activation for that old path in this PR.

## Behavioral Proof

`nix/checks/openclaw-runtime-path.nix` builds a Nix-only probe command and runs `nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-path-smoke.mjs`.

That smoke does all of the following:

- evaluates generated Home Manager config and proves `tools.exec.pathPrepend` includes the probe package bin dir;
- inspects the generated gateway wrapper and proves it exports Codex app-server PATH args without overriding `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN`;
- imports the packaged OpenClaw Codex runtime plugin and verifies the app-server start options remain `commandSource = "managed"`;
- starts the packaged Codex app-server command path and runs `command/exec`;
- runs a negative control where isolated Codex config masks inherited wrapper `PATH`, and the probe command fails;
- runs the positive case with generated `shell_environment_policy.set.PATH`, and the probe command resolves from the Nix store path and prints the expected output.

The rebuilt check prints this proof summary in the Nix log:

```text
openclaw-runtime-path proof:
- generated tools.exec.pathPrepend includes openclaw-runtime-path-probe
- wrapper exports Codex app-server PATH args without overriding the managed Codex binary
- Codex command/exec fails when isolated Codex config masks inherited wrapper PATH
- Codex command/exec succeeds when generated PATH resolves /nix/store/...-openclaw-runtime-path-probe/bin/openclaw-runtime-path-probe
```

## CI Wiring Proof

`flake.nix` includes `runtime-path` inside `checks.<system>.runtime-smoke`.

`.github/workflows/ci.yml` builds `runtime-smoke` in both supported-surface jobs:

- `Linux supported surface`: `.#checks.x86_64-linux.runtime-smoke`
- `macOS supported surface`: `.#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-smoke`

The same workflow also builds platform activation on Linux and builds/runs the macOS launchd activation smoke. The activation smokes assert that the wrapper contains `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS`, contains `shell_environment_policy.set.PATH=`, includes the runtime package path, and does not contain `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN`.

## Local Proof Run

Run on the current branch before pushing this update:

```text
nix fmt --accept-flake-config -- CHANGELOG.md nix/modules/home-manager/openclaw/config.nix nix/checks/openclaw-runtime-path.nix nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-path-smoke.mjs nix/tests/hm-activation.py scripts/hm-activation-macos.sh
node --check nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-path-smoke.mjs
nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link --rebuild --print-build-logs .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-path
nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link --print-build-logs .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-smoke .#checks.aarch64-darwin.module-render
nix build --accept-flake-config --out-link result-platform-activation-local .#checks.aarch64-darwin.platform-activation
OPENCLAW_HM_ACTIVATION_PACKAGE="$PWD/result-platform-activation-local" scripts/hm-activation-macos.sh
nix eval --accept-flake-config .#checks.x86_64-linux.runtime-path.drvPath
nix eval --accept-flake-config .#checks.x86_64-linux.runtime-smoke.drvPath
nix eval --accept-flake-config .#checks.x86_64-linux.platform-activation.drvPath
git diff --check
```

Two read-only review agents also checked the updated code paths: one traced nix-openclaw to OpenClaw's managed Codex app-server resolver and Codex `shell_environment_policy.set.PATH`, and one checked proof/CI wiring. Both reported no blockers. The exact pushed head still needs GitHub Actions to prove Linux and macOS CI on this commit.
target.author: joshp123

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
